channel.title: ShadowFetch News
channel.description: Late-breaking headlines across every beat — aggregated, organized, and brief-first.

item.title: Bucks waive guard Thomas to convert Nance
item.description: The Bucks waived guard Cam Thomas to convert the contract of forward Pete Nance into a standard deal.
item.author: ESPN Headlines

item.title: Udoka calls out 'soft' officiating in Rockets' loss
item.description: Rockets coach Ime Udoka blasted the officiating after he was ejected from his team's loss to the Bulls, saying they made "soft" calls.
item.author: ESPN NBA

item.title: Lakers laud resiliency in losing effort to Pistons
item.description: The Lakers failed to extend their nine-game winning streak but still lauded their effort in rallying from a 16-point deficit in the second half.
item.author: ESPN NBA

item.title: Kerr: Will play Curry in play-in tourney if healthy
item.description: Golden State coach Steve Kerr dismissed the notion that the Warriors would sit Stephen Curry without a guaranteed spot in the playoffs.
item.author: ESPN NBA

item.title: OKC's Williams returns: Wrist finally 100%, too
item.description: Jalen Williams made his return to the Thunder lineup Monday after missing 26 of the prior 28 games with a hamstring strain and said the time off helped his surgically repaired w...
item.author: ESPN NBA

item.title: Mexican prospect Lopez declares for NBA draft
item.description: Karim Lopez, who spent two seasons in the Australian NBL, has declared for the NBA draft and is poised to become the first player born in Mexico to be picked in the first round.
item.author: ESPN NBA

item.title: 🏀 Ranking best, worst moves since last summer
item.description: Bradley Beal to the Clippers? Jared McCain to the Thunder!? We ranked the best and worst moves since last offseason.
item.author: ESPN NBA

item.title: NBA draft stock watch: Latest buzz, scouting reports on 21 top prospects
item.description: Which 2026 NBA draft prospects helped or hurt their stock after an exciting first weekend during March Madness?
item.author: ESPN NBA

item.title: How Jaxon Smith-Njigba's contract extension impacts Seahawks, Rams' Puka Nacua
item.description: Answering five questions about the record-breaking contract extension for the NFL's top receiver in 2025.
item.author: ESPN NFL

item.title: NBA playoff watch: Nuggets, Suns face off in key West battle
item.description: Can Phoenix make up ground in the West standings? Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after Monday.
item.author: ESPN NBA

item.title: Predicting Rounds 1-2 of the NFL draft: Yates finds landing spots for 64 top prospects
item.description: Field Yates unveils his latest NFL mock draft, projecting two full rounds. Which top prospects could go in the opening 64 picks?
item.author: ESPN Headlines

item.title: Kiper's new NFL mock draft: How free agency shook up first-round landing spots
item.description: Team needs have shifted in free agency, so how will top prospects come off the board in Round 1?
item.author: ESPN NFL

item.title: Nuki adds Apple Home Key to its smart lock
item.description: This week, my top pick for a retrofit smart lock launched the first Aliro-compatible keypad. The Nuki Keypad 2 NFC ($179) brings tap-to-unlock via Apple's Home Key and Samsung's...
item.author: The Verge

item.title: Dame Shirley Bassey reveals she can no longer reply to fan mail
item.description: The singer, 89, said she was 'truly humbled' by the letters she had received during her 70-year career.
item.author: BBC News

item.title: NASA Unveils Initiatives to Achieve America’s National Space Policy
item.description: As part of its “Ignition” event on Tuesday, NASA announced a series of transformative agencywide initiatives designed to achieve President Donald J. Trump’s National Space Polic...
item.author: NASA Artemis

item.title: World Snooker Championship to remain at Crucible until 2045 as £45m revamp planned
item.description: The redevelopment of the Sheffield venue will, though, see the tournament temporarily relocated from 2028.
item.author: The Athletic

item.title: How to watch Minnesota Twins: 'Field of Dreams' game, TV channels to know in 2026
item.description: The Twins are looking to the future as they begin the 2026 season. Here's how to watch all of their national and regional games this year.
item.author: The Athletic

item.title: Revamped Crucible to host World Championship until 2045
item.description: The World Snooker Championship will remain at the Crucible in Sheffield until 2045, with the venue set to be refurbished to add up to 500 additional seats.
item.author: BBC Sport

item.title: Global oil prices are back above $102 a barrel after Saudi Arabia and U.A.E. reportedly weigh joining Iran war
item.description: U.S. allies in the Persian Gulf are reportedly inching toward joining the fight against Iran, pushing oil prices higher.
item.author: MarketWatch

item.title: TikTok ads are about to get a tad more disruptive
item.description: One new ad format displays another brand's logo alongside TikTok's on the launch page when users open the app.
item.author: TechCrunch

item.title: Mirage raises $75M to continue building models for its AI video editing app Captions
item.description: Mirage, the maker of video editing app Captions, has raised $75 million in growth financing from General Catalyst's Customer Value Fund (CVF).
item.author: TechCrunch

item.title: Spotify’s new SongDNA feature maps how your favorite songs are connected
item.description: The new feature lets you explore samples, covers, and more about the people behind your favorite songs, says Spotify.
item.author: TechCrunch

item.title: Snapchat’s new ‘AI Clips’ Lens format turns photos into five-second videos
item.description: Snapchat says both experienced and new developers can use the new "AI Clips" Lens format to turn a single prompt into a published Lens in minutes without the need for external t...
item.author: TechCrunch

item.title: Zoox brings its robotaxis to Austin and Miami
item.description: After nearly two years of testing in the two cities, the Amazon-owned company says it's getting closer to offering rides.
item.author: TechCrunch

item.title: Cauldron Ferm has turned microbes into nonstop assembly lines
item.description: The Australian startup says it has solved one of the biggest challenges facing synthetic biology companies, which it counts as customers.
item.author: TechCrunch

item.title: Arriving safely on Venus and Titan: Different atmospheres affect heat shields differently, study shows
item.description: Heat shields keep incoming spacecraft from burning up in planetary atmospheres. Now we're learning that different atmospheres have different effects.
item.author: Space.com

item.title: TV Academy Reveals New Eligibility Rules for Prop Masters
item.description: Property masters are now eligible for active voting or associate membership within the Television Academy. The Academy created a new Property Masters subgroup within the Directo...
item.author: Variety

item.title: Roku’s Howdy $3 Monthly Streamer Now Available Through Amazon’s Prime Video
item.description: Roku has lassoed Amazon’s Prime Video Channels as a distributor of its low-cost, no-ads Howdy streaming service, marking the first time the $2.99/month package is available outs...
item.author: Variety

item.title: 11 Designer Toiletry Bags So Good, You’ll Want to Carry Them to Dinner
item.description: As handbag prices surge, are luxury dopp kits the next big thing?
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: For ‘You, Me & Tuscany,’ the Location Did Half the Work
item.description: Halle Bailey, Regé-Jean Page and director Kat Coiro flew halfway around the world to make a romantic comedy. Turns out Italy had the romantic part covered.
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: Camp Crafts? Not the Vibe. At These Resorts, the Kids Are Getting Facials
item.description: Some high-end hotels are expanding spa menus for children and teens, with offerings that include acne facials, mango scrubs and other early introductions to the wellness-industr...
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: Hollywood’s Ultimate Travel Luxury: Privacy
item.description: Forget St. Barts. Entertainment’s most well-traveled are ditching the Cabo-Amalfi circuit for destinations where no one knows their name — and that’s exactly the point.
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: That Time Matt Bomer Lost His Passport in Japan
item.description: The 'White Collar' actor — soon to be seen in Jonah Hill's upcoming Apple TV film 'Outcome' — on cherry blossom season, staying at the 'Lost in Translation' hotel and losing his...
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: How Luxury Travel Is Going Overboard for Superyachts
item.description: High-end hospitality brands — like Aman, Four Seasons, The Ritz-Carlton and Orient Express — are betting that travelers who hate traditional cruises will happily climb aboard th...
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: Wanna Beat the Summer Heat? Check Out These Cool European Hotspots
item.description: With temperatures expected to sizzle this summer to over 100 degrees in Southern Europe, savvy Hollywood travelers will be chilling out in the Alps, Scandinavia and Scotland.
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: The Big Life in Big Sky Country: What to Do in Montana
item.description: Janie Bryant, the fashion force behind such Taylor Sheridan dramas as '1883,' '1923' and now 'The Madison,' shares her favorite outposts in and near Bozeman for shopping, eating...
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: For Daniel Dae Kim, Korea Is Personal
item.description: The host of CNN’s upcoming travel show “K-Everything” tells THR about returning to the country he left as a baby — and how he ended up being named an honorary citizen.
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: Neil Patrick Harris Wants to Murder Your Next Vacation
item.description: At Little Palm Island in Florida, the actor’s immersive luxury weekend turns a five-star getaway into a live-action whodunit — and offers a glimpse into celebrity travel’s next...
item.author: Hollywood Reporter

item.title: ‘It’s Personality Theft’: How Creators Are Fighting Back Against AI Deepfakes
item.description: As artificial intelligence becomes more accessible, podcasters, TikTokers, and other creators are finding replicas of themselves promoting companies they've never even heard of
item.author: Rolling Stone

item.title: Solana Foundation taps Mastercard, Western Union, Worldpay for institutional developer platform
item.description: The platform is a toolkit that lets enterprises create and scale financial applications on Solana without deep crypto infrastructure expertise.
item.author: CoinDesk
